root_text.sn15.7:1.2: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yena bhagavā …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn15.7:1.3: “kīvabahukā nu kho, bhante, kappā abbhatītā atikkantā”ti?
root_text.sn15.7:1.6: ‘ettakā kappā iti vā, ettakāni kappasatāni iti vā, ettakāni kappasahassāni iti vā, ettakāni kappasatasahassāni iti vā’”ti.
root_text.sn15.7:2.4: Te divase divase kappasatasahassaṁ kappasatasahassaṁ anussareyyuṁ.
root_text.sn15.7:2.5: Ananussaritāva, bhikkhave, tehi kappā assu, atha kho te cattāro sāvakā vassasatāyukā vassasatajīvino vassasatassa accayena kālaṁ kareyyuṁ.
root_text.sn15.7:2.6: Evaṁ bahukā kho, bhikkhave, kappā abbhatītā atikkantā.
root_text.sn15.7:2.8: ‘ettakā kappā iti vā, ettakāni kappasatāni iti vā, ettakāni kappasahassāni iti vā, ettakāni kappasatasahassāni iti vā’ti.

translation_text.sn15.7:1.2: Then several mendicants went up to the Buddha … and asked him,
translation_text.sn15.7:1.6: how many eons have passed, how many hundreds or thousands or hundreds of thousands of eons.”
translation_text.sn15.7:2.1: “But sir, is it possible to give a simile?”
translation_text.sn15.7:2.3: “Suppose there were four disciples with a lifespan of a hundred years.
translation_text.sn15.7:2.4: And each day they would each recollect a hundred thousand eons.
translation_text.sn15.7:2.5: Those four disciples would pass away after a hundred years and there would still be eons that they haven’t recollected.
translation_text.sn15.7:2.6: That’s how many eons have passed.
translation_text.sn15.7:2.8: how many eons have passed, how many hundreds or thousands or hundreds of thousands of eons.
translation_text.sn15.7:2.10: This transmigration has no known beginning. …
translation_text.sn15.7:2.11: This is quite enough for you to become disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed regarding all conditions.”
